title: Calepina irregularis
intro: Calepina irregularis, also known as rock cabbage or wild mustard, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its compact growth and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health concerns. The bioactive constituents of Calepina irregularis include glucosinolates, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Calepina irregularis is commonly prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is used to treat gastrointestinal problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Calepina irregularis is a member of the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This family includes a wide range of plants, many of which are commonly consumed as vegetables. Calepina irregularis is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in dry, rocky areas. It is a perennial herb with a spreading habit and is known to produce small, white or pale yellow flowers. The plant's classification within the Brassicaceae family is further specified as being within the genus Calepina.
